title: T-062 — UI: PLN-026 Sequencing Apply Confirm modal (parity with sequencing_apply_confirm_modal)
prompt:
Implement UI: PLN-026 Sequencing Apply Confirm modal (parity with sequencing_apply_confirm_modal) for 04-PLANNING-BASIC.

Prototype: `_meta/prototype-labels/prototype-index-planning.json` label `sequencing_apply_confirm_modal` -> `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/planning/modals.jsx:1053-1100`. Read PRD refs §10.5, §10.7 and UX refs before coding. Match the prototype structurally and visually using shadcn/Radix primitives, Next.js App Router, Server Components for initial data, Server Actions for writes, next-intl for all copy, and existing repository conventions.

Business rules: Apply action is destructive/reordering; server action audits old/new sequence and leaves critical exemptions intact.

Closeout must include changed files, test commands/results, AC status, screenshot/artifact paths, Playwright trace paths, deviations, and git status. Do not implement out-of-scope modules; use cross-module contracts listed in pipeline_inputs.cross_module_dependencies.

pipeline_inputs.description: Sequencing apply confirmation modal with blast-radius summary and acknowledgement.
pipeline_inputs.details: Prototype reference: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/planning/modals.jsx:1053-1100. Apply action is destructive/reordering; server action audits old/new sequence and leaves critical exemptions intact.
out_of_scope: Using D365 as source-of-truth for factory usability

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Planning settings/workflow/status/RBAC UI consumes Settings rule registry, flags, and permission contracts.

acceptance_criteria: Given the surface is implemented, when compared to `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/planning/modals.jsx:1053-1100`, then layout, copy hierarchy, components, interactions, empty/loading/error states, and accessibility affordances have prototype parity for `sequencing_apply_confirm_modal`.
Given server data or validation returns blocked/empty/error cases, then the UI surfaces the exact PRD validation/status messages without client-invented business state.
Given closeout is prepared, then it includes desktop screenshots/artifacts for all material states plus Playwright trace artifacts for happy path and blocked/error path.

Given tests run, then targeted RTL/component tests and Playwright e2e for the route/modal pass with 0 failures. Follow parity evidence requirements in `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`.
test_strategy: RED RTL/component test for prototype parity and validation states.
Playwright e2e covers route/modal open, happy path, blocked/error path, and records trace.
Run targeted pnpm vitest and pnpm playwright commands.
risk_red_lines: Do not hardcode strings; use next-intl.
Do not bypass server-side RBAC/business validation.
Do not infer factory usability from D365 or latest BOM/spec outside canonical release read model.
ui_closeout_evidence: Desktop screenshots/artifacts for all material states.
Playwright trace artifacts for happy path and blocked/error path.
